does: This paper introduces MUSE, a comprehensive framework addressing multi-turn jailbreak vulnerabilities in large language models through both attack and defense mechanisms. MUSE-A implements a novel attack method leveraging frame semantics and heuristic tree search to systematically explore diverse semantic trajectories across conversation turns, enabling adversaries to craft context-dependent manipulations that bypass safety mechanisms. MUSE-D presents a defense strategy using fine-grained safety alignment that intervenes early in dialogues to preemptively reduce vulnerabilities before harmful patterns emerge. The framework is evaluated across multiple LLMs, demonstrating its effectiveness at both identifying and mitigating multi-turn jailbreak risks through systematic experimentation.
connects: This work builds on established red teaming literature for LLMs while addressing a critical gap in existing research. Prior safety work has concentrated on single-turn interactions and prompt-level attacks, but MUSE responds to the reality that deployed models encounter multi-turn conversations where context accumulation enables more sophisticated attacks. The paper extends adversarial attack methodologies by incorporating tree search algorithms similar to those used in game-playing AI and planning problems, combined with linguistic insights from frame semantics. It also connects to dialogue-based defense strategies and safety fine-tuning approaches, synthesizing these lines to create an integrated attack-defense evaluation framework designed specifically for conversational vulnerabilities.
enables: MUSE establishes a foundation for future work in conversational AI safety by providing both a systematic attack methodology and defense mechanism for multi-turn scenarios. The framework enables more rigorous evaluation of LLM robustness in realistic deployment contexts where conversations naturally extend across multiple turns. Follow-up research can build on the tree search approach to develop more sophisticated adversarial strategies, explore the interplay between early intervention points and overall dialogue safety, and extend the framework to longer conversations and more complex narrative structures. The work also points toward developing defense mechanisms that maintain helpfulness while preventing jailbreaks, and toward automated evaluation systems that can assess multi-turn safety at scale during model development and deployment.
